title: ZRC-ND Phase D biological follow-up package
objective: Determine whether ZRC-ND-conditioned medium that has passed non-cell chemistry gates preserves neural culture health, morphology, and functional activity well enough to support a first suitability claim.

scope.entry_condition: Run only after Phase A/B/C non-cell gates pass for the lead article.
scope.sample_type: media or conditioned-media proxy exposed to the ZRC-ND lead, unmodified 3.5 kDa baseline, 10 kDa challenge, and no-module controls
scope.claim_after_success: Supports suitability for biological conditioned-medium pilot use, not final long-duration CL1 deployment.
scope.not_in_scope: Regulatory approval, chronic manufacturing validation, direct cell-contact material certification, and organismal safety.

run_matrix.name: Extract or conditioned-medium viability screen
run_matrix.purpose: Detect acute material-conditioned medium toxicity before morphology or network claims.

run_matrix.name: Neurite and morphology screen
run_matrix.purpose: Check whether ZRC-ND-conditioned medium preserves neural morphology and outgrowth relative to no-module control.

run_matrix.name: Network activity stability screen
run_matrix.purpose: Check whether ZRC-ND-conditioned medium preserves MEA or equivalent functional network activity relative to no-module control.

assay_panel.readout: Metabolic viability and LDH/cell lysis relative to no-module control
assay_panel.method_class: metabolic viability assay plus LDH or equivalent cytotoxicity assay

assay_panel.readout: Neurite length/branching and cell-body count relative to no-module control
assay_panel.method_class: high-content imaging or equivalent neural morphology assay

assay_panel.readout: MEA spike rate, burst rate, and synchrony relative to no-module control
assay_panel.method_class: MEA or equivalent functional network activity assay

acceptance_gates.criterion: Lead-conditioned medium keeps metabolic viability >= 90 percent of no-module control and LDH/cell lysis <= 120 percent of no-module control.
acceptance_gates.failure_response: Do not claim suitability; investigate extractables, coating residues, medium drift, or cartridge preconditioning.

acceptance_gates.criterion: Lead-conditioned medium keeps neurite length and branching >= 85 percent of no-module control and cell-body count >= 90 percent.
acceptance_gates.failure_response: Do not claim neural suitability; repeat with lower exposure, extra rinsing, or simplified unmodified RC baseline.

acceptance_gates.criterion: Lead-conditioned medium keeps spike rate, burst rate, and synchrony within 70 to 130 percent of no-module control, unless a domain-specific assay defines a tighter gate.
acceptance_gates.failure_response: Do not claim CL1-like functional suitability; preserve the candidate as chemistry-compatible only.

acceptance_gates.criterion: Lead is not worse than unmodified 3.5 kDa baseline on viability/morphology and is safer than the 10 kDa challenge on functional or morphology retention.
acceptance_gates.failure_response: Reconsider whether the coating route adds value or whether the MWCO challenge is too disruptive.
